IEEE ICDM 2026

The world's premier research conference in Data Mining

November 12-15, 2026

Shenyang, China

IEEE ICDM 2026

The world's premier research conference in Data Mining

November 12-15, 2026

Shenyang, China

IEEE ICDM 2026

The world's premier research conference in Data Mining

November 12-15, 2026

Shenyang, China

Welcome to ICDM 2026

The IEEE International Conference on Data Mining (ICDM) has established itself as the world's premier research conference in data mining. It provides an international forum for sharing original research results, as well as for exchanging and disseminating innovative and practical development experiences. The conference covers all aspects of data mining, including algorithms, software, systems, and applications.

ICDM draws researchers, application developers, and practitioners from a wide range of data mining-related areas, such as big data, deep learning, pattern recognition, statistical and machine learning, databases, data warehousing, data visualization, knowledge-based systems, high-performance computing, and large models. By promoting novel, high-quality research findings and innovative solutions to challenging data mining problems, the conference seeks to advance the state of the art in data mining.


NEW IN 2026 Applied Track — ICDM 2026 introduces a dedicated Applied Track welcoming papers on real-world deployed systems, interdisciplinary applications, new datasets, and comprehensive experimental analyses.
Deadline: June 6, 2026 View Call for Papers →

Topics of Interest

Topics of interest include, but are not limited to:

Foundations & Theories

Foundations, algorithms, models, and theory of data mining, including big data mining.

ML/DL & Statistical Methods

Machine learning, deep learning, and statistical methods for big data.

Heterogeneous Data Mining

Mining heterogeneous data sources, including text, semi-structured, spatio-temporal, streaming, graph, web, and multimedia data.

Systems & Platforms

Data mining systems and platforms for analyzing big data, including methods for parallel and distributed data mining, federated learning, and their efficiency, scalability, security, and privacy.

Modeling, Visualization & Recommendations

Data mining for modeling, visualization, personalization, and recommendation.

CPS & Complex Time-Evolving Networks

Data mining for cyber-physical systems and complex, time-evolving networks.

LLM-Driven

Data mining with large language models.

Novel Applications

Novel applications of data mining in data science, including big data analysis in social sciences, physical sciences, engineering, life sciences, climate science, web, marketing, finance, precision medicine, health informatics, and other domains.

We particularly encourage submissions in emerging topics of high importance, such as ethical data analytics, automated data analytics, data-driven reasoning, interpretable modeling, modeling with evolving environments, multi-modal data mining, and heterogeneous data integration and mining.

Shenyang — Where Heritage Meets Innovation

Shenyang is a city where deep historical roots meet a driving momentum for innovation — from its UNESCO-listed Imperial Palace to its thriving modern skyline, a destination of infinite dimensions awaits. We thank you for participating and look forward to seeing you in Shenyang, China!

News & Updates

2026-03-24

Call for PhD Forum Applications Launched

ICDM 2026 launches the PhD Forum, providing a platform for PhD students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career researchers to present ongoing work, receive feedback, and explore career opportunities. Submissions are due June 26, 2026.

2026-03-08

Call for Demo Papers Launched

ICDM 2026 invites submissions to the Demo Track, which provides a platform for researchers and practitioners to showcase innovative, fully implemented data mining systems through interactive demonstrations. Demo papers are limited to 4 pages in IEEE 2-column format and will be reviewed under a single-blind process. The submission deadline is August 20, 2026.

2026-02-05

Call for Education Forum Papers Launched

ICDM 2026 launches the Education Forum, inviting submissions on innovative approaches to teaching and learning data mining and AI. The track focuses on pedagogical methods, curriculum design, experiential learning, and educational data mining.

2026-02-04

Call for Applied Track Papers Released

For the first time, ICDM 2026 adds a brand-new Applied Track alongside the existing Research Track. It welcomes submissions on real-world data mining applications, industrial systems, interdisciplinary studies, new datasets, and comprehensive algorithm analysis. Manuscripts follow the IEEE two-column format with a 10-page limit under single-blind review.

2026-02-03

Calls for Workshops, and Tutorials, and BlueSky Track Papers Launched

ICDM 2026 simultaneously launches calls for Workshops and Tutorials. Workshop proposals are due March 14, 2026; Tutorial proposals are due June 6, 2026, aiming to enrich the conference content and foster in-depth exchanges.

2026-02-02

Key Dates Updated

The important ICDM deadlines have been confirmed. Please plan your submissions accordingly.

2026-01-30

Call for Teen Research Symposium Launched

The Call for Teen Research Symposium page is now live in both English and Chinese. It is a dedicated forum for high school students interested in data mining to present original research.

2025-12-28

Call For Sponsorship Launched

The Call for Sponsorship page is now live in both English and Chinese. We warmly invite industry partners, research institutions, and organizations to support the conference and engage with the global data mining community.

2025-12-15

Call for Research Track Papers Released

ICDM 2026 opens its call for original research papers spanning all data mining fields, including algorithms, deep learning, large models, multi-modal mining and diverse applications. Submissions follow IEEE two-column format within 10 pages under strict triple-blind review rules.

2025-11-11

Homepage Launched

The official homepage of ICDM 2026 is now live. Relevant pages and supporting information will be updated gradually.

2026-03-24

Call for PhD Forum Applications Launched

ICDM 2026 launches the PhD Forum, providing a platform for PhD students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career researchers to present ongoing work, receive feedback, and explore career opportunities. Submissions are due June 26, 2026.

2026-03-08

Call for Demo Papers Launched

ICDM 2026 invites submissions to the Demo Track, which provides a platform for researchers and practitioners to showcase innovative, fully implemented data mining systems through interactive demonstrations. Demo papers are limited to 4 pages in IEEE 2-column format and will be reviewed under a single-blind process. The submission deadline is August 20, 2026.

2026-02-05

Call for Education Forum Papers Launched

ICDM 2026 launches the Education Forum, inviting submissions on innovative approaches to teaching and learning data mining and AI. The track focuses on pedagogical methods, curriculum design, experiential learning, and educational data mining.

2026-02-04

Call for Applied Track Papers Released

For the first time, ICDM 2026 adds a brand-new Applied Track alongside the existing Research Track. It welcomes submissions on real-world data mining applications, industrial systems, interdisciplinary studies, new datasets, and comprehensive algorithm analysis. Manuscripts follow the IEEE two-column format with a 10-page limit under single-blind review.

2026-02-03

Calls for Workshops, and Tutorials, and BlueSky Track Papers Launched

ICDM 2026 simultaneously launches calls for Workshops and Tutorials. Workshop proposals are due March 14, 2026; Tutorial proposals are due June 6, 2026, aiming to enrich the conference content and foster in-depth exchanges.

2026-02-02

Key Dates Updated

The important ICDM deadlines have been confirmed. Please plan your submissions accordingly.

2026-01-30

Call for Teen Research Symposium Launched

The Call for Teen Research Symposium page is now live in both English and Chinese. It is a dedicated forum for high school students interested in data mining to present original research.

2025-12-28

Call For Sponsorship Launched

The Call for Sponsorship page is now live in both English and Chinese. We warmly invite industry partners, research institutions, and organizations to support the conference and engage with the global data mining community.

2025-12-15

Call for Research Track Papers Released

ICDM 2026 opens its call for original research papers spanning all data mining fields, including algorithms, deep learning, large models, multi-modal mining and diverse applications. Submissions follow IEEE two-column format within 10 pages under strict triple-blind review rules.

2025-11-11

Homepage Launched

The official homepage of ICDM 2026 is now live. Relevant pages and supporting information will be updated gradually.

Important Dates

  • Main Conference Full Paper Submission: June 6, 2026
  • Main Conference Author Notification: August 16, 2026
  • Workshop Paper Submission: August 20, 2026
  • Workshop Paper Notifications: September 18, 2026
